comments.author: WrightBackWhereIBelong
comments.text: WRIGHT IN AN ASTON MARTIN GT3 WITH RED BULL LIVERY. At the Nürburgring. Against Väisänen, MaraDöner, and Thompson in a Porsche. The race is set for 16–17 May — just four weeks away — and this is the crossover event of the decade. The man won at Daytona, he's an LMP2 champion, and now he's taking on the Nordschleife in the same class as the championship leader. The shakedown at COTA in that white Red Bull livery is a flex — no green, just full Red Bull branding, a nod to his Racing Bulls ties. And Springfield in the same car? After she beat him at Daytona, this is the rematch setup we didn't know we needed. Lundqvist adds Indy 500 pedigree. This Aston Martin entry is stacked, and May can't come soon enough.

replies.author: NASCARtoF1
replies.text: Wright's endurance background — LMP2 champion, IMSA winner, Daytona 500 top-ten — makes him the most qualified F1 driver for the Nordschleife. He's raced prototypes, stock cars, and now a GT3 Aston Martin. The only thing missing is a rally car and a dirt bike. The Red Bull livery at COTA is a nice touch — Racing Bulls colours on an Aston Martin, with an Indy 500 winner in the third seat. Four weeks until they take on the Green Hell.

replies.author: SpringfieldStan
replies.text: SPRINGFIELD IN AN ASTON MARTIN AT THE NORDSCHLEIFE! This is what she deserves! She's been carrying that F1 team all season — 24 of their 24 points — and now she gets to share a GT3 car with a world champion and an Indy 500 winner. The Porsche trio has the championship leader; the Aston trio has the endurance specialist and the chaos queen. 16–17 May is going to be incredible.

comments.author: GridGazer
comments.text: The Nürburgring 24H on 16–17 May is turning into an unofficial F1CC mid-season championship. On one side: Väisänen, MaraDöner, and Thompson in a Porsche. On the other: Wright, Springfield, and Lundqvist in an Aston Martin. In the same class. Head-to-head through the Nordschleife. Rossi's piece confirms what we suspected — this isn't just a fun weekend outing. This is a competitive entry in the premier GT3 category against a rival F1CC-connected crew. The Porsche trio has the championship leader and two of the grid's most promising talents. The Aston trio has a world champion, the grid's most underrated driver, and an Indy 500 winner. The bragging rights alone are enormous. Plus Keisner, Vettel, and Koopman in the SPX Audi wagon confirmed back in March. The N24 is going to have more F1 talent than some grand prix weekends.

replies.author: PaddockInsider
replies.text: The class structure makes this even better. The Porsche and Aston Martin are both in SP9 Pro — the top GT3 class. That means they'll be racing directly against each other for position, not just lapping the Nordschleife as a demonstration. Keisner's Audi wagon with Vettel and Koopman is in SPX (experimental), so that's a separate battle. But the Porsche vs Aston Martin fight is a proper race. Väisänen vs Wright, head-to-head, through the most dangerous circuit in the world. Four weeks from now. What could possibly go wrong?

replies.author: ChaosReigns
replies.text: The F1CC paddock at the N24 on 16–17 May: Väisänen/MaraDöner/Thompson in a Porsche, Wright/Springfield/Lundqvist in an Aston Martin, Keisner/Vettel/Koopman in an Audi wagon. That's seven current F1 drivers, one team principal (TEF is racing in F1 but not in this), one four-time world champion, and one Indy 500 winner. The Nordschleife is going to need a bigger paddock. Mid-May is circled in permanent marker on my calendar.

comments.author: SpringfieldStan
comments.text: Sarah Springfield sharing a car with an Indy 500 winner and a world champion is the recognition she's been denied all season. She's scored every point for Aston Martin in F1 — 24 of 24 — and now she gets to race at the Nordschleife in May in the same class as the championship leader. The Porsche trio is stacked with youth and speed. The Aston trio is stacked with experience and grit. Springfield's wet-weather skills and chaos management are tailor-made for the Nordschleife. If the Eifel weather turns — and in mid-May it often does — she could be the difference-maker.

replies.author: DNF_Fakt
replies.text: Springfield gets to share a GT3 car with a world champion and an Indy 500 winner for the N24. Fakt gets to share an F1 garage with a car that occasionally finishes. The contrast is staggering. At least she'll have a capable teammate for one weekend in May. Lundqvist won the Indy 500 — that's not a consolation prize, that's a statement co-driver.

replies.author: FaktLap1Legend
replies.text: The GOAT doesn't need the Nordschleife in May. Sam Fakt has already ascended beyond the physical realm of GT3 cars, station wagons, and Porsches. While these mortals battle through the Green Hell, Fakt will be meditating on the astral plane, preparing for the F0 championship that will render all of this irrelevant. 🐐

replies.author: DNF_Fakt
replies.text: He's not meditating. He's probably reviewing telemetry from his last DNF. Springfield is at the Nordschleife in May. Wright is at the Nordschleife. Keisner is at the Nordschleife. Vettel is coming out of retirement for it. Fakt is... where exactly?

comments.author: IndyCarCrossover
comments.text: Linus Lundqvist in an F1CC-connected N24 entry is a great call. The guy won the 2025 Indy 500, has IMSA endurance experience, and knows how to handle high-pressure situations. Wright, Springfield, and Lundqvist is a trio with zero weak links. The Porsche might have the championship leader, but the Aston Martin has the most balanced lineup of any SP9 Pro entry. Lundqvist's Indy 500 experience — 500 miles of oval racing — might seem irrelevant to the Nordschleife, but the mental stamina required for both is comparable. Four weeks until we see this lineup in action.

replies.author: NASCARtoF1
replies.text: Wright and Lundqvist both have IndyCar connections — Wright via his IMSA days, Lundqvist as an Indy 500 winner. The crossover between American open-wheel racing and this Aston Martin entry is fascinating. Red Bull branding ties it all together. This is a genuinely international effort: an American world champion, a British underdog, and a Swedish Indy 500 winner in a British car with Austrian energy drink colours, all converging on Germany in May.

comments.author: MaraDönerFan42
comments.text: The Porsche vs Aston Martin battle on 16–17 May is going to be personal. MaraDöner and Wright have been fighting in the F1 midfield all season. Väisänen and Springfield have history from China — she led the race before her spin. Thompson is Väisänen's protégé. These aren't strangers sharing a track; they're rivals who see each other every race weekend. The Nordschleife in mid-May is going to feel like an extension of the F1 paddock, except with 170 corners and no run-off. I cannot wait.

replies.author: GreekLightning
replies.text: Ο Μιχάλης σε Porsche στο Nordschleife τον Μάιο! Θα τους δείξει όλους! 🇬🇷

comments.author: TifosiForever
comments.text: Väisänen in a Porsche GT3 at the Nordschleife in May, head-to-head against Wright in an Aston Martin. This is going to be a fascinating subplot to the F1 season. The championship leader taking on a former world champion in a completely different discipline. Väisänen has been flawless in F1 this season; let's see how he handles the Green Hell. Forza Ferrari — even in a Porsche.

replies.author: FlyingFinn53
replies.text: MY BOY EETU in a PORSCHE at the NORDSCHLEIFE in MAY! He's going to DESTROY everyone! Wright is great but EETU is UNSTOPPABLE! The Green Hell has never seen a Finn like this! KIITOS EETU!! 🇫🇮

replies.author: RealityCheck
replies.text: The Nordschleife is a completely different discipline from F1. Wright has more endurance experience — LMP2 champion, IMSA winner, Daytona 500. Väisänen is the better F1 driver, but the 'Ring rewards experience. This won't be a walkover for either side when they line up in May.

comments.author: AudiRising
comments.text: The N24 entry list for 16–17 May is becoming a who's who of 2026 motorsport. Keisner/Vettel/Koopman in an Audi RS6 Avant GT3 (SPX). Väisänen/MaraDöner/Thompson in a Porsche 911 GT3 R (SP9 Pro). Wright/Springfield/Lundqvist in an Aston Martin Vantage GT3 (SP9 Pro). That's three entries featuring a combined nine drivers with F1, F2, IndyCar, and WEC pedigree. The Nürburgring has always been special, but this year it's becoming a de facto all-star race in mid-May. The only question is which entry will claim bragging rights. My money is on the Audi — Vettel's experience and Keisner's current form are a lethal combination, even if the wagon is in a different class.

replies.author: FourRingsOrBust
replies.text: The RS6 Avant GT3 is the wildcard. It's an experimental estate car — nobody knows what it'll do over 24 hours. But with Vettel's experience and Keisner's pace, they'll extract everything it has. The Porsche and Aston Martin will be racing each other in SP9 Pro; the Audi will be racing the BMW M3 Touring in SPX. Different classes, same mountain in May. The marketing value alone is staggering.

comments.author: SbinnalaEnjoyer
comments.text: A Porsche with the championship leader and two young stars. An Aston Martin with a world champion, an underrated ace, and an Indy 500 winner. An Audi wagon with a four-time F1 champion, a current race winner, and a recovering rookie. The Nürburgring 24H on 16–17 May is not a race anymore. It's a festival of 'who can assemble the most absurd driver lineup.' And I am here for every single entry.

comments.author: N24Fanatic
comments.text: As someone who goes to the Nürburgring 24H every year, I can tell you the paddock is already buzzing about these entries for mid-May. The SP9 Pro class is always competitive, but having two F1CC-connected crews go head-to-head is unprecedented. The Porsche camp vs the Aston Martin camp — it's going to feel like a proxy war. And the fans are going to love it. The Nordschleife doesn't care about your championship position. It just eats you alive if you're not ready.

comments.author: StatsNerd_26
comments.text: Combined stats of the three N24 F1CC-connected entries for 16–17 May — Porsche: Väisänen (64 F1CC points, 2 wins), MaraDöner (27 points, 1 podium), Thompson (0 F1CC points, F2 winner). Aston Martin: Wright (1 F1 title, 14 points), Springfield (24 points, 4 finishes), Lundqvist (1 Indy 500 win). Audi: Keisner (1 win, 41 points), Vettel (4 F1 titles, 53 wins), Koopman (3 F1CC points). Total across all three entries: 5 F1 world championships, 54 F1 wins, 3 current F1CC race wins, 1 Indy 500 win. The Nordschleife is hosting a hall of fame induction ceremony disguised as a motor race.

comments.author: COTASpotter
comments.text: I was at COTA for Wright's shakedown. That Red Bull livery on the Aston Martin is stunning — white with the full Red Bull branding, not a trace of Aston green. The car sounded incredible, and Wright looked completely at ease. He was chatting with the engineers between runs, probably diagnosing axle runouts or something. The guy just understands machinery. If the Aston is fast at the Nordschleife in May, Wright will be the reason why.
